subject: Migrate oom docs
commit_message:
Migrate oom docs

Merging this will create
https://onap-oom.readthedocs.io
This build will run in parallel
to the current docs generation method
until we are ready to cut over.

Issue-ID: CIMAN-364
Signed-off-by: Jessica Wagantall <jwagantall@linuxfoundation.org>
Change-Id: I502ea73223a2fe2f7f8d235a3544a186ae3d45c1

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Hi Jess,

I've already asked this question in review for OSA but let me do this again.

Are you sure that copy-pasting all those files into ~100 different repos is the best what we can do? What happens if we need to fix sth in any of those?
Uploaded patch set 2: New patch set was added with same tree, parent, and commit message as Patch Set 1.

Patch Set 2:

I'm not going to give +1 or +2 on this patch.
I still believe that copying the same files to dozen of repos is a terrible design but as most of projects already merged this I'm not going to block this

@Sylvain
Fortunately I'm not the PTL so I don't need to make hard decisions;)
It's up to you to decide whether to take this patch or not
I don't like the patch but if you want to give it a chance give +2 and merge it with my -1 (gerrit blocks merges only with -2)

Patch Set 2:

The new method gives each project its own proper read the docs project, attaching it to the master read the docs project via subproject and intersphinx linking. so it is a necessary evil. (if you want to escape from submodules, which is essentially how these files are getting included at the moment)
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2
